author: Chanakya (Kautilya)
description: Ancient Indian treatise on statecraft, economic policy, and military strategy

teachings.text: Before you start a war, be it with your enemy or with yourself, understand where power lies.
The enemy of my enemy is my friend. Build alliances based on mutual interests, not emotions.
A leader should keep his plans secret. Share them only when the time for execution is right.
The treasury is the root of all prosperity. Without wealth, all other objectives become impossible.
Never make important decisions when you are angry or desperate. Wait until your mind is clear.
The wise leader employs spies to understand the intentions and capabilities of both enemies and allies.
Power is of three kinds: the power of counsel, the power of treasury, and the power of the military. The greatest of these is the power of counsel.
The king who is slow in punishing becomes powerless. The king who is too quick in punishing becomes feared. The wise king balances mercy with firmness.
